title: Rhodanthemum maresii
taxonomy: Rhodanthemum maresii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places Rhodanthemum maresii within the order Asterales, which comprises flowering plants with composite flowers. As a member of Asteraceae, Rhodanthemum maresii is further grouped into the subfamily Carduoideae, a subfamily of daisies and thistles. Within this subfamily, Rhodanthemum maresii is placed in the tribe Cardueae. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Rhodanthemum maresii within its plant family.

intro: Rhodanthemum maresii, also known as Mares' Whiskers, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhodanthemum maresii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rhodanthemum maresii is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and other gastrointestinal issues.
